# Tensorgami

Tensorgami is Henry Shin's public mathematical workbench for proof notes,
expository reconstructions, problem sets, Lean formalizations, and explicit
proof mechanisms. Some entries record new arguments, extraction
formulas, or finite certificates; others rebuild known results to make the
mechanism explicit.
Individual entries identify contribution type locally, distinguishing new
constructions, independent solutions, expository reconstructions, problem
sets, and Lean formalizations.

The archive emphasizes normal forms, reductions, obstructions, finite
certificates, algorithmic extraction, concrete decompositions, descent, and
selective formal verification.

## Main pages

- https://tensorgami.com/
- https://tensorgami.com/mechanisms.html
- https://tensorgami.com/verification.html
- https://tensorgami.com/about.html
- https://tensorgami.com/privacy.html
- https://tensorgami.com/license.html

## Selected artifacts

### A Weighting Argument for Pointwise Convergence to Convergence in Measure

- Page: https://tensorgami.com/notes/pointwise-weighting-convergence-in-measure.html
- PDF: https://tensorgami.com/pdfs/analysis/A_Weighting_Argument_for_Pointwise_Convergence_to_Convergence_in_Measure.pdf
- Lean source: https://github.com/Tensorgami/tensorgami.github.io/blob/main/formalizations/pointwise-weighting/PointwiseWeighting/Basic.lean
- Contribution type: new construction; Lean formalization.
- Contribution: explicit measurable-weight construction upgrading almost-everywhere convergence on a sigma-finite measure space to conull uniformization and convergence in measure after multiplying by a strictly positive measurable weight.
- Lean theorem: `PointwiseWeighting.exists_conull_pos_measurable_weight_uniformOn_tendstoInMeasure_of_ae_tendsto`
- Real-line theorem: `PointwiseWeighting.real_exists_conull_pos_measurable_weight_uniformOn_tendstoInMeasure`
- Mechanisms: Egorov pieces, pointwise envelope, measurable cover-index weight, convergence in measure.

### IBM Ponder This July 2010

- Page: https://tensorgami.com/notes/ibm-ponder-this-2010.html
- PDF: https://tensorgami.com/pdfs/number-theory/A_Galois_Theoretic_Solution_to_the_July_2010_IBM_Ponder_This_Challenge.pdf
- Lean source: https://github.com/Tensorgami/tensorgami.github.io/blob/main/formalizations/ibm-ponder-this-2010/IBMPonderThis/Main.lean
- Contribution type: independent solution; Lean formalization.
- Contribution: independent trace and companion-matrix solution to the July 2010 IBM challenge, with the final finite modular certificate verified in Lean.
- Lean theorem: `IBMPonderThisFinal.ibm_ponder_this_main`
- Mechanisms: Pisot rounding, trace recurrence, companion-matrix period, finite modular certificate.

### Divisibility of Separated Polynomials

- Page: https://tensorgami.com/notes/separated-polynomial-divisibility.html
- PDF: https://tensorgami.com/pdfs/algebra/A_Divisibility_Criterion_for_Separated_Polynomial_Differences.pdf
- Lean source: https://github.com/Tensorgami/tensorgami.github.io/blob/main/formalizations/separated-polynomial-differences/SeparatedPolynomialDifferences.lean
- Contribution type: new argument; Lean formalization.
- Contribution: common-outer-polynomial divisibility criterion for separated polynomial differences, proved through free-module residue expansion and diagonal reduction.
- Lean theorem: `SeparatedPolynomialDifferences.sep_dvd_iff_exists_common_outer`
- Mechanisms: free-module residue expansion, diagonal reduction, descent.

### A Galois-Theoretic and Frobenius-Descent Viewpoint on a Polynomial Composition Law

- Page: https://tensorgami.com/notes/galois-frobenius-composition-law.html
- PDF: https://tensorgami.com/pdfs/algebra/A_Galois_Theoretic_and_Frobenius_Descent_Viewpoint_on_a_Polynomial_Composition_Law.pdf
- Lean source: https://github.com/Tensorgami/tensorgami.github.io/blob/main/formalizations/galois-frobenius-composition-law/GaloisFrobeniusCompositionLaw.lean
- Contribution type: new viewpoint; Lean formalization.
- Contribution: Galois-descent and Frobenius-reduction viewpoint on a separated-polynomial composition criterion, including a Lean-checked cubic square-root functional equation.
- Lean theorem family: `SeparatedComposition.separated_composition_criterion_iff`
- Mechanisms: generic fibers, Galois descent, Frobenius reduction.

### Normality, Coherent Extension, Tensor Operations, and Affineness

- Page: https://tensorgami.com/notes/hartshorne-ii-5-14-18.html
- PDF: https://tensorgami.com/pdfs/algebraic-geometry/Notes_on_Hartshorne_Algebraic_Geometry_Chapter_II_Section_5_Exercises_14_18.pdf
- Contribution type: independent exercise solutions.
- Contribution: independent solutions to Hartshorne Algebraic Geometry Chapter II Section 5 Exercises 14-18, with emphasis on projective normality, coherent extension, tensor operations, affine morphisms, relative Spec, and vector bundles.
- Mechanisms: normalization, section rings, Veronese bookkeeping, geometric mechanism.

### Extracting the First Prime Greater than m

- Page: https://tensorgami.com/notes/extracting-first-prime.html
- PDF: https://tensorgami.com/pdfs/number-theory/Extracting_the_First_Prime_Greater_than_m.pdf
- Lean source: https://github.com/Tensorgami/tensorgami.github.io/blob/main/formalizations/extracting-first-prime/ExtractingFirstPrime.lean
- Contribution type: new construction; Lean formalization.
- Contribution: explicit finite extraction formula for the least prime above `m`, with the final correctness theorem verified in Lean.
- Lean theorem: `ExtractingFirstPrime.extractedPrime_is_smallest_prime_gt`
- Mechanisms: gcd filtering, valuation selection, finite certificate.

### A Note on Disjoint Decompositions of [0,1) into Closed Sets

- Page: https://tensorgami.com/notes/disjoint-decompositions.html
- PDF: https://tensorgami.com/pdfs/analysis/A_Note_on_Disjoint_Decompositions_of_0_1_into_Closed_Sets.pdf
- Lean source: https://github.com/Tensorgami/tensorgami.github.io/blob/main/formalizations/disjoint-decomposition/DisjointDecomposition.lean
- Contribution type: independent solution; Lean formalization.
- Contribution: independent proof of the non-decomposition result via endpoint obstruction and Baire category, with the final statement formalized in Lean.
- Lean theorem: `no_pairwiseDisjoint_closed_iUnion_eq_Ico`
- Mechanisms: compactification, boundary obstruction, Baire category.

### Lebesgue's Criterion for Riemann Integrability

- Page: https://tensorgami.com/notes/lebesgue-riemann-integrability.html
- PDF: https://tensorgami.com/pdfs/analysis/Lebesgue_s_Criterion_for_Riemann_Integrability.pdf
- Contribution type: expository reconstruction.
- Contribution: topological proof of the sufficiency direction, organized around compact neighborhoods of the discontinuity set and finite partition control.
- Mechanisms: compactness, quarantining bad sets, finite partition.

### Selected Problems in Discrete Mathematics and Analysis

- Page: https://tensorgami.com/notes/selected-problems-discrete-mathematics-analysis.html
- PDF: https://tensorgami.com/pdfs/problem-sets/Exercise_Problems_in_Mathematics.pdf
- TeX source: https://tensorgami.com/tex/problem-sets/Selected_Problems_in_Discrete_Mathematics_and_Analysis.tex
- Contribution type: problem set.
- Contribution: web edition of a compact problem set in counting, covering systems, recurrences, asymptotic series, and finite information encoding.
- Mechanisms: stars and bars, gap transformations, finite-state recurrences, asymptotic cancellation, ternary outcome coding.

## Selected problem records

### Bilkent Mathematical Problem of the Month, November 2008

- Source: https://math.bilkent.edu.tr/Problem/problem_2008.html
- Problem: https://math.bilkent.edu.tr/Problem/0810q.pdf
- Official solution: https://math.bilkent.edu.tr/Problem/0811a.pdf
- Record: one of three listed solvers for the November 2008 white-sequence problem.
- Planned Tensorgami work: exposition and Lean formalization.
- Mechanisms: self-referential recurrence, finite-state dynamics, eventual periodicity, extremal sequence problem.

### IBM Ponder This, July 2010

- Source: https://research.ibm.com/blog/ponder-this-july-2010
- Tensorgami page: https://tensorgami.com/notes/ibm-ponder-this-2010.html
- Record: listed solver.
- Tensorgami work: exposition and Lean formalization available.
- Mechanisms: Pisot rounding, trace recurrence, companion-matrix period, finite modular certificate.

### IBM Ponder This, August 2010

- Source: https://research.ibm.com/blog/ponder-this-august-2010
- Record: listed solver for the Lilliput ID-number challenge.
- Planned Tensorgami work: exposition and Lean formalization.
- Mechanisms: extremal finite configurations, coordinate projections, finite certificates.

## Formalization environment

The formalized artifacts are independent Lake projects, each pinned to its own
Lean toolchain and mathlib revision. GitHub Actions runs `lake build` for each
formalization project.

## External identifiers

- GitHub: https://github.com/Tensorgami
- ORCID: https://orcid.org/0009-0000-6453-6865
- LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/in/henry-k-shin/

- Contact: hkshin@alumni.harvard.edu
